text.heading: Fake merchant navy groups seize Nigeria’s waters
text.p: THE Nigerian Merchant Navy under the umbrella of Nigerian Merchant Navy Officers and Water Transportation Senior Staff Association has raised alarm over the emergence of faceless groups masquerading as merchant navy groups.
Already one of the leaders of the groups, a welder with the defunct Nigerian National Shipping Line (NNSL) who posed as a Commodore Benson Edema has been arrested by the Nigerian Navy.

The President of the association, Captain Thomas Kemewerigha said two illegal groups have been posing as merchant navy. He listed them as including the Merchant Navy and Petroleum Monitoring Group which is headed by Edema and the Professional Seafarers Group operating at Niger Delta.
He said the association was worried over the grave security implication the development would have on the the nation.

He wondered why groups like Merchant Navy and Petroleum Monitoring Corp headed by a self-styled Commodore Benson Edema were allowed to operate despite its attempt to alert the necessary quarters until he was arrested by the Navy in Lagos few weeks ago.
Another group Professional Seafarers is operating in the Niger Delta extorting money from the unsuspecting public.
At a press conference, Kemewerigha said the Merchant Navy is always one body in every country and that it is not a para-military group but usually committed to the welfare of seamen.

"This body was first discovered in Abuja and we being the custodian of the Nigerian Merchant Navy alerted the authorities but no action was taken until the Navy acted. We don’t want to believe that some powerful people in government are behind this group as they are using government property as their secretariat. We want full investigation by the police on this as the property is still being opened daily in Lagos for this group to continue to operate Kemewerigha told journalists at the association’s office in Lagos at the weekend.

Commending the effort of the Nigerian Maritime Administration and Safety Agency (NIMASA) for being able to register Nigeria into the white list of the International Maritime Organisation (IMO), he said the International Transport Federation (IMO), the world body for seafarers, had enquired about the illegal groups since, according to him, there could not be more than one as it will jeopardise Nigeria’s membership of the world maritime organisation.

Kemewerigha said the leader of the said Merchant Navy Corp is a pensioner of the defunct Nigeria National Shipping Line where he was disengaged as a welder and wondered how he became an officer, decorating himself as a Commodore, a rank which does not exist in the Merchant Navy.
Kemewerigha noted that Civil Liberty Organisation (CLO) has not been properly educated on the issue before condemning the arrest and detention of Benson Edema.
